entities.name: PHOSPHATE
entities.description: A chemical nutrient (P) whose adsorption-desorption characteristics are studied in soils.

entities.name: PHOSPHATE ADSORPTION-DESORPTION
entities.description: The process of phosphate binding to and releasing from soil particles.

entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: A type of clay-rich soil characterized by high shrink-swell capacity.

entities.name: PHOSPHATE ADSORPTION CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of soil to adsorb phosphate, measured in ug g-1 of soil.

entities.name: LANGMUIR EQUATION
entities.description: A mathematical model used to describe the adsorption of phosphate in soils.

entities.name: FREUNDLICH EQUATION
entities.description: A mathematical model used to describe the adsorption of phosphate in soils.

entities.name: GUNNARY EQUATION
entities.description: A mathematical model used to describe the adsorption of phosphate in soils.

entities.name: TEMPKIN EQUATION
entities.description: A mathematical model used to describe the adsorption of phosphate in soils.

entities.name: DUBININ-RADUSHKEVICH EQUATION
entities.description: A mathematical model used to describe the adsorption of phosphate in soils.

entities.name: DTPA EXTRACTABLE FE
entities.description: The amount of iron extractable by DTPA, used as a correlate for phosphate adsorption.

entities.name: CLAY CONTENT
entities.description: The proportion of clay particles in the soil.

entities.name: P FERTILIZATION
entities.description: The application of phosphate fertilizers to soil.
